nodes.name: gan_reconstruction
nodes.description: Reconstructs a clean ECG-like signal from a noisy PPG input using a pre-trained GAN generator. Accepts a clean PPG signal and additive noise, feeds them through the generator network on the specified device, and returns the reconstructed output signal.

inputs.name: ppg_clean
inputs.type_desc: array-like | Tensor — clean PPG waveform segment(s)
inputs.constraints: shape compatible with generator input; values typically normalized

inputs.name: noise
inputs.type_desc: array-like | Tensor — additive noise to condition or corrupt the input
inputs.constraints: same shape as ppg_clean; treated as frozen/static stochastic input

inputs.name: sampling_rate
inputs.type_desc: int | float — signal sampling rate in Hz
inputs.constraints: positive; determines temporal resolution of the waveform

inputs.name: generator
inputs.type_desc: nn.Module | callable — pre-trained GAN generator network
inputs.constraints: stateless inference only; weights frozen during call

inputs.name: device
inputs.constraints: generator and tensors must reside on same device

outputs.name: reconstructed_signal
outputs.constraints: same temporal length as input; values in generator output range

nodes.type_signature: (ppg_clean: array-like | Tensor — clean PPG waveform segment(s), noise: array-like | Tensor — additive noise to condition or corrupt the input, sampling_rate: int | float — signal sampling rate in Hz, generator: nn.Module | callable — pre-trained GAN generator network, device: str | torch.device — compute device (e.g. 'cpu', 'cuda')) -> array-like | Tensor — GAN-reconstructed signal
nodes.conceptual_summary: frozen_adversarial_manifold_projector. A pre-trained generative adversarial decoder maps a corrupted or composite input signal — formed by superimposing a structured reference waveform with a stochastic noise tensor — onto a learned target-domain manifold, producing a denoised or cross-modal reconstruction of identical temporal dimensionality. The generator network, whose weights are fully frozen, acts as a deterministic oracle over a learned latent-to-signal mapping, effectively performing a conditional nonlinear projection from a noisy observation space into a cleaner, structurally regularized output space. The noise tensor functions as a fixed stochastic conditioning variable that parameterizes the generative transform rather than being filtered away, making this a noise-conditioned signal-morphing operation rather than a classical filter.. Applications: Astrophysics — Reconstructing clean stellar flux light curves from telescope photometry contaminated by atmospheric turbulence and instrument shot noise, using a GAN trained on synthetic stellar variability models to project noisy cadence-sampled observations onto a physically plausible emission manifold, Seismology — Translating surface-recorded seismic waveforms (corrupted by anthropogenic noise or sensor drift) into estimated subsurface ground-motion signatures by conditioning a frozen generative decoder on the raw trace plus a static noise realization, enabling cross-modal waveform domain transfer, Materials Science — Mapping noisy electron microscopy scan-line signals (e.g., STEM intensity profiles corrupted by beam instability) to denoised, structurally regularized 1-D lattice profiles by projecting the composite input through a GAN generator trained on idealized crystallographic simulations, Neuroimaging — Converting low-SNR single-trial EEG epoch tensors, additively corrupted by muscle artifacts modeled as frozen noise, into reconstructed cortical source-space time series via a pre-trained adversarial decoder that has learned the manifold of clean evoked-response waveforms

nodes.name: initialize_reconstruction_state
nodes.description: Set up reconstruction loop parameters: compute the per-iteration reconstruction length from the shifting step and sampling rate, and initialize the reconstructed noise accumulator and remaining noise counter.
nodes.type_signature: (noise: list, sampling_rate: int | float) -> tuple[int, list, int]

nodes.name: normalize_and_batch_clean_segment
nodes.description: Scale the current clean PPG segment to zero-mean/unit-variance, then tile it into a 256-sample batch and convert to a FloatTensor ready for the generator.

nodes.name: generate_reconstructed_segment
nodes.description: Feed the batched clean segment through the pre-trained GAN generator on the target device and extract the first reconstructed noise waveform from the batch output.

outputs.name: noise_rec
outputs.constraints: 1-D waveform, first sample of generator batch output

nodes.type_signature: (ppg_test: torch.FloatTensor, generator: nn.Module | callable, device: str | torch.device) -> np.ndarray

nodes.name: detect_peaks_in_reconstructed_and_clean
nodes.description: Upsample both the reconstructed noise segment and the clean PPG segment, then detect systolic peaks in each using a minimum-distance criterion derived from the sampling rate.
nodes.type_signature: (noise_rec: np.ndarray, ppg_clean: np.ndarray, sampling_rate: int | float) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray]

nodes.name: stitch_clean_and_reconstructed_waveforms
nodes.description: Concatenate the clean PPG up to its last detected peak with the reconstructed noise from its first detected peak onward, then downsample the stitched waveform back to the combined original-length sample count.

inputs.name: ppg_clean
inputs.constraints: original (non-upsampled) clean segment, used for length reference

inputs.name: noise_rec
inputs.constraints: original (non-upsampled) reconstructed segment, used for length reference

outputs.name: ppg_and_rec_down
outputs.constraints: downsampled stitched waveform of length len(ppg_clean)+len(noise_rec)

nodes.type_signature: (ppg_resampled: np.ndarray, peaks_ppg: np.ndarray, noise_rec_res: np.ndarray, peaks_rec: np.ndarray, ppg_clean: np.ndarray, noise_rec: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray

nodes.name: accumulate_reconstructed_noise_and_advance_window
nodes.description: Slice the downsampled stitched signal to extract the newly reconstructed noise portion (full step or remaining tail), append it to the accumulator, update the remaining noise counter, and advance the clean-segment window for the next iteration.

inputs.name: ppg_clean
inputs.constraints: current clean segment (for length reference and next-window extraction)

outputs.name: reconstructed_noise
outputs.constraints: updated accumulator with newly appended samples

outputs.name: remaining_noise
outputs.constraints: decremented by len_rec, or unchanged on final partial step

outputs.name: ppg_clean_next
outputs.constraints: next clean segment window advanced by len_rec samples

nodes.type_signature: (ppg_and_rec_down: np.ndarray, ppg_clean: np.ndarray, reconstructed_noise: list, remaining_noise: int, len_rec: int) -> tuple[list, int, np.ndarray]
